label: Squarish 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED with mid-band HALL, LD style — bedrooms and the common T&B all open onto a hallway tucked into the middle band between master (front) and standard (rear). Hall has an open mouth east into dining. Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split stacked front-to-rear, not a combined great room.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — interior door from master, west side of the bath block. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
shared plumbing wall — wet stacks in one bay. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common's north face touches standard — bath block is the acoustic+privacy buffer between bedrooms. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
master door opens north into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + 2x 0.10 m frame stops. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common bath door opens east into the hall — 0.8 m bath leaf + frame. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
standard door opens south into the hall — 1.0 m = 0.8 m clear + frame. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining (no door — codebase rule: hallway<->public is always open). dining, not living, because the hall sits in the MIDDLE band between master and standard -- its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row. Tested hall->living directly: infeasible even at 11x11 (wrong row). Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling.
living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).
dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.

soft_proximities.note: keep the master-adjacent side of the wet core close to master. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Unchanged from the gr sibling -- tried loosening both auto_apply floors (kitchen 1.8->1.6, hallway 0.95->0.9) on the LDK draft to chase gr's smaller 9.5x9.5 floor; true floor stayed at 9.9x9.9 regardless, confirming the extra ~0.4 m the LDK split costs is a genuine structural cost of the hall->dining connection (dining's width has to satisfy both the hall's mouth AND its own living/kitchen stacking), not a tunable profile threshold. Reverted to the gr sibling's original values.

notes: Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked
front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same
day, this topology replaces it. Private column + hall structure
entirely unchanged: master front / standard rear, ensuite+common wet
core and a hallway both tucked into the middle band, bedroom and bath
doors all open onto the hall (never directly onto the public side).
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):
Front: [master                              | living                   ]
Mid:   [ensuite | common  | HALL            | dining                   ]
Rear:  [standard                            | kitchen                  ]
Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves
from great_room to dining (not living) -- the hall sits in the MIDDLE
band between master and standard, so its wall has to align with
whichever public room occupies that same middle row. Verified this
empirically: hall->living tested directly and is infeasible even at
11x11 (wrong row), hall->dining solves cleanly. living gets
hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room, same role).
Published minimum: 10x10 (true floor ~9.9x9.9, confirmed by
bisection). This is WORSE than the gr sibling's 9.5x9.5 floor --
unlike cl_gr->cl_ld (smaller floor) and bath_gr->bath_ld (same floor
after re-tuning), the LDK split here costs about 0.4 m of minimum lot
size. Tried loosening the compact-shell profile's auto_apply floors
(kitchen 1.8->1.6, hallway 0.95->0.9) to chase parity with gr; true
floor stayed at 9.9x9.9 regardless, so the gap is a genuine structural
cost of dining's width needing to satisfy both the hall's mouth AND
its own living/kitchen stacking, not a tunable threshold. Kept the
profile at its original (gr) values since loosening didn't help.
At 10x10 and 11x11/12x12 (the published sweep sizes), warning counts
are IDENTICAL to the gr sibling at the same sizes (1 warn
tiered_preferred_dropped at 10x10, 0 warn at 11x11 and up) --
suggestion counts run slightly higher (more distinct rooms to flag),
cosmetic only.
fallback_topology unchanged from the gr sibling -- still points to
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld.json (confirmed dormant, never
triggered by any existing brief, same as before).
